note: Populated sample export for frontend integration testing. Covers all coordinateQuality and locationType values, all required event categories. Replace with live export output from `npm run export` in production.

events.title: Iran IRGC seizes Marshall Islands tanker in Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: IRGC naval forces boarded and detained a Marshall Islands-flagged crude carrier in international waters near the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel is carrying 2.1 million barrels of Iraqi crude bound for South Korea. Iran cited unspecified maritime law violations. US Fifth Fleet has deployed an escort destroyer to the area.

events.title: Houthi ballistic missile disables two berths at Ras Tanura loading terminal
events.summary: A Houthi ballistic missile struck the Ras Tanura oil export terminal on the Saudi eastern coast, disabling two of eight loading berths. Aramco confirmed a temporary halt to export operations from the affected infrastructure. Ras Tanura is the world's largest oil-loading facility, handling approximately 6.5% of global seaborne crude exports.

events.title: Iran signals Hormuz closure if nuclear talks collapse
events.summary: Senior IRGC commander issued a formal statement warning that Iran would 'consider all options including restricting transit through the Strait of Hormuz' if nuclear negotiations with the P5+1 fail to produce a framework by month end. Hormuz carries approximately 21 million barrels per day, roughly 20% of global oil trade.

events.title: Suez Canal tanker runs aground at km 95, halts northbound traffic
events.summary: A 300,000 DWT VLCC grounded at kilometer 95 of the Suez Canal while transiting northbound, blocking traffic for 14 hours. The Suez Canal Authority deployed six tugboats and successfully refloated the vessel with no cargo spill. Northbound traffic was delayed by an average of 18 hours; 67 vessels queued during the closure.

events.title: Turkey closes Bosphorus Strait to tankers for 36 hours due to dense fog
events.summary: Turkish maritime authorities closed the Bosphorus Strait to tanker traffic for 36 hours due to dense fog reducing visibility to under 100 meters. Approximately 45 tankers, including 12 VLCCs carrying Russian Urals crude, were delayed. Turkey applies strict fog closure rules under its 1994 maritime traffic regulations.

events.title: Piracy incident on Malacca Strait tanker prompts international response
events.summary: Armed pirates boarded a Singapore-flagged chemical tanker in the Malacca Strait, 40 nautical miles southwest of Lumut, Malaysia. The vessel was carrying 45,000 MT of palm oil derivatives. Pirates held the crew for 11 hours before abandoning the vessel. The ReCAAP Information Sharing Centre categorized the incident as Category 1 piracy.

events.title: Yemen Houthis threaten resumed Red Sea tanker attacks within 72 hours
events.summary: The Houthi military spokesperson announced it would resume attacks on all vessels transiting the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden if Israeli military operations in Gaza continued beyond 72 hours. The group halted attacks in March following a ceasefire. Shipping rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope has added $1.8M per voyage in fuel and time costs.

events.title: PKK bomb disables Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline, halting 450kbpd of northern Iraqi exports
events.summary: Kurdish PKK militants detonated an explosive device on the Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline near Dohuk Governorate, rupturing a 42-inch section and forcing an immediate shutdown. Iraq's North Oil Company halted approximately 450,000 bpd of exports through the line. The pipeline has suffered 19 attacks since 2020; repairs typically take 10–21 days.

events.title: Russian drone strikes Druzhba pipeline pumping station in Stryi, Ukraine
events.summary: Ukrainian authorities confirmed a Russian drone strike hit a Druzhba oil pipeline pumping station near Stryi, Lviv Oblast, temporarily suspending westward crude flows through the southern branch of the pipeline serving Hungary, Slovakia, and the Czech Republic. The affected section carries approximately 300,000 bpd to central European refineries.

events.title: Mexico Cantarell platform fire forces 180kbpd production halt
events.summary: A fire broke out on the Cantarell B-2 production platform in the Bay of Campeche, forcing Pemex to shut in approximately 180,000 bpd of production. Two workers were injured; no fatalities reported. Pemex's fire suppression team extinguished the blaze within six hours. Production restart timeline is estimated at 15–20 days pending safety inspection.

events.title: Saudi Aramco Abqaiq processing fire — controlled within four hours
events.summary: A fire broke out at a gas-oil separation plant at Abqaiq, the world's largest crude oil processing facility, responsible for approximately 7% of global supply. The fire was caused by a mechanical failure in a heat exchanger. Aramco contained the blaze within four hours with no reported impact on crude output capacity. Production remained at normal rates throughout.

events.title: Colombia Caño Limón pipeline bombed by ELN guerrillas
events.summary: National Liberation Army (ELN) militants detonated explosives at two points on the Caño Limón–Coveñas pipeline in Norte de Santander Department, forcing Ecopetrol to shut down the line carrying approximately 70,000 bpd. This is the 11th attack on the pipeline in 2026. Cleanup and repairs are expected to take 8–12 days.

events.title: Ecuador Trans-Ecuadorian pipeline suspended after landslide in Napo Province
events.summary: A landslide triggered by heavy rainfall in Napo Province severed the Trans-Ecuadorian pipeline (SOTE) and the OCP heavy crude pipeline, halting approximately 400,000 bpd of exports. PetroEcuador declared force majeure on crude lifting commitments. Historical landslides in the area have caused closures of 10–30 days.

events.title: Syria SDF seizes Conoco gas plant and Deir ez-Zor oilfields from pro-government forces
events.summary: Syrian Democratic Forces recaptured the Conoco gas plant and three adjacent oilfields in Deir ez-Zor Governorate from pro-government militias following three days of fighting. The fields collectively produce approximately 12,000 bpd. Control of Syrian oil revenue has been a persistent flashpoint between the SDF and Damascus-backed groups.

events.title: DRC armed group sabotages SOCAP pipeline in Bas-Congo province
events.summary: Armed militants sabotaged a section of the Société Congolaise des Pétroles pipeline in Bas-Congo Province, forcing a production shutdown at two onshore oilfields producing approximately 20,000 bpd. The DRC's oil output is modest but strategically important for government revenue. Security forces have been deployed to the area.

events.title: OPEC+ calls emergency virtual meeting to discuss 500kbpd additional cut
events.summary: Saudi Arabia called an emergency virtual meeting of OPEC+ after Brent crude fell below $72 per barrel for the first time since November. Saudi Arabia is seeking an additional 500,000 bpd cut from voluntary producers, against resistance from Russia and the UAE. Meeting is scheduled for 48 hours hence; consensus is uncertain.

events.title: Saudi Arabia announces voluntary output cut extension through Q3 2026
events.summary: Saudi Arabia's energy ministry confirmed it will extend its voluntary 1 million bpd output cut through Q3 2026, citing the need to support market stability. The cut, first introduced in July 2025, is unilateral and in addition to agreed OPEC+ quota reductions. Saudi production will remain at 8.95 million bpd for at least the next three months.

events.title: Iraq OPEC+ quota compliance falls to 74% as southern output surges
events.summary: Secondary source surveys indicate Iraq's OPEC+ compliance fell to 74% in April, as output from Basra Oil Terminal reached 3.8 million bpd against its agreed quota of approximately 3.3 million bpd. Iraq's oil ministry disputed the figures. Persistent Iraqi overproduction has been a source of intra-OPEC tension and has undermined the group's price management credibility.

events.title: US Treasury designates 12 tankers in largest Iran shadow-fleet enforcement action
events.summary: The US Treasury's OFAC designated 12 tankers and three shipping companies involved in transporting Iranian crude to Chinese refineries, in the largest single enforcement action targeting Iran's shadow fleet. Treasury estimates the action could reduce Iranian export capacity by 200,000–400,000 bpd. Iran denied the designations have any operational effect.

events.title: EU extends Russia oil price cap enforcement, adds 18 vessels to evasion list
events.summary: The European Union extended its Russian oil price cap enforcement regime and added 18 tankers to the list of vessels suspected of carrying Russian crude above the $60/barrel cap. The move comes amid reports that Russian shadow fleet operators have increasingly used ship-to-ship transfers in international waters off Ceuta to circumvent price cap monitoring.

events.title: US adds PDVSA subsidiary to SDN list, tightening Venezuela crude export controls
events.summary: OFAC designated PDV Holding Inc., a PDVSA subsidiary managing European and US asset operations, adding it to the Specially Designated Nationals list. The action targets the subsidiary's role in facilitating crude sales through third-country intermediaries. Existing Chevron licenses are not affected, but spot market participants are reassessing compliance risk.

events.title: Syria oil export prohibition extended; EU renews blanket ban for 12 months
events.summary: The European Union extended its prohibition on Syrian crude and petroleum product imports for a further 12 months, covering all 27 member states. The ban applies to oil originating from territory controlled by the Syrian government and its affiliated entities. Syrian oil exports are minimal but the measure sends a political signal ahead of UN negotiations.

events.title: Libya NOC declares force majeure at Sharara oilfield after armed blockade
events.summary: Libya's National Oil Corporation declared force majeure on Sharara oilfield liftings after armed groups from the Petroleum Facilities Guard blockaded the field's entry points. Sharara, Libya's largest oilfield, produces approximately 300,000 bpd. NOC said it cannot guarantee export commitments while the blockade persists. International buyers are seeking alternate Libyan crudes.

events.title: Nigeria NNPC confirms force majeure on Bonny Light liftings after terminal flooding
events.summary: NNPC confirmed force majeure on Bonny Light crude liftings following flooding of key facilities at Bonny Island export terminal caused by heavy seasonal rainfall. Bonny Light accounts for approximately 200,000 bpd of Nigerian exports. Infrastructure damage assessment is ongoing; timeline for resumption is unclear. Buyers are diverting to Forcados and Qua Iboe grades.

events.title: Venezuela PDVSA restores Lake Maracaibo output after 36-hour power outage
events.summary: PDVSA restored operations at three crude separation platforms on Lake Maracaibo following a 36-hour power outage that forced production cuts of approximately 120,000 bpd. Venezuela's national grid remains critically fragile; analysts warn of recurring outages ahead of the rainy season. Cumulative outage-related production losses in 2026 have reached an estimated 18 million barrels.

events.title: Kazakhstan Tengiz compressor failure cuts output by 100kbpd
events.summary: Chevron reported a 15% reduction in output at Kazakhstan's Tengiz field — the country's largest — following a compressor failure in one of three processing trains. The field typically produces 680,000 bpd. Repairs are expected to take 10–14 days with full output restored by late May. The Caspian Pipeline Consortium, which transports Tengiz crude to Novorossiysk, reported unchanged throughput from buffer storage.

events.title: Iraq Basra crude export terminal hit by structural damage, loading cut by 40%
events.summary: Iraq's Basra Oil Terminal suffered structural damage to a single-point mooring buoy system following severe Gulf storm conditions, reducing loading capacity by approximately 40% at the country's primary crude export point. The terminal normally handles 1.6–1.8 million bpd. Emergency repairs are underway; alternate berths are absorbing some volume.

events.title: IEA coordinates emergency oil release of 60 million barrels from member reserves
events.summary: The International Energy Agency announced a coordinated emergency release of 60 million barrels from strategic petroleum reserves held by member countries, the fifth such collective action in IEA history. The release, spread over 30 days, is intended to offset supply losses from simultaneous disruptions in Libya, Iraq, and Venezuela. The US, Japan, and Germany are the largest contributors.

events.title: EIA weekly report shows surprise 8.2M barrel US crude inventory build
events.summary: The US Energy Information Administration weekly petroleum status report showed a surprise 8.2 million barrel build in commercial crude inventories, well above the consensus estimate of a 1.5 million barrel draw. The build was attributed to higher imports from Canada (up 450kbpd) and reduced refinery utilization due to seasonal maintenance shutdowns.

events.title: Sudan Greater Nile Pipeline shutdown extends amid RSF-SAF conflict
events.summary: The Greater Nile Oil Pipeline, which carries Sudanese crude to Port Sudan for export, remains shut after fighting between the Rapid Support Forces and the Sudanese Armed Forces damaged a pumping station in White Nile State. The pipeline previously exported approximately 130,000 bpd. South Sudan, which relies on the pipeline to access world markets, faces mounting revenue losses estimated at $2M per day.

events.title: Norway Johan Sverdrup production platform shuts down for emergency maintenance
events.summary: Equinor confirmed an unplanned shutdown of the Johan Sverdrup Phase 1 production platform after a fire detection system triggered an automatic shutdown. The platform normally produces 535,000 bpd, making Johan Sverdrup Europe's largest oilfield. Emergency inspection teams are on site; restart is expected within 4–6 days pending safety certification.

events.title: North Sea storm forces four UK UKCS platforms to halt production
events.summary: A severe North Sea storm with 12-meter wave heights and 60-knot winds forced four UK Continental Shelf operators to temporarily evacuate platforms and halt production. Combined shut-in volume is estimated at 180,000 bpd across platforms operated by BP, Shell, and EnQuest. Platforms are expected to resume operations within 48–72 hours as weather improves.

events.title: UAE ADNOC announces 500kbpd capacity expansion in offshore Umm Shaif field
events.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Company announced a $3.2 billion investment to expand production capacity at the offshore Umm Shaif field by 500,000 bpd by 2029, as part of the UAE's strategy to reach 5 million bpd nameplate capacity by 2030. The project will add two new wellhead towers and a floating storage and offloading unit.

events.title: Angola Block 48 deepwater development plan approved for accelerated timeline
events.summary: Angola's National Agency for Oil, Gas and Biofuels approved TotalEnergies' revised development plan for Block 48, a deepwater discovery estimated to hold 500 million barrels of recoverable reserves. First oil is now projected for 2029, two years ahead of the original schedule. Angola is prioritizing deepwater development to offset declines from mature onshore fields.

events.title: Brazil Petrobras sets deepwater production record at 3.2 million bpd
events.summary: Petrobras confirmed a new pre-salt deepwater production record of 3.2 million bpd, surpassing the previous record set in January. The Santos Basin pre-salt cluster contributed 2.1 million bpd, led by the Búzios megafield. Petrobras maintained its 2026 production guidance of 2.9–3.1 million bpd total. Brazil has become the world's seventh-largest oil producer.

events.title: Kuwait KNPC Al-Zour refinery offline after heat exchanger rupture
events.summary: Kuwait National Petroleum Company's Al-Zour refinery — the Middle East's largest refinery complex at 615,000 bpd — is operating at reduced capacity after a heat exchanger rupture in one of its crude distillation units. KNPC has isolated the unit and reduced throughput by approximately 80,000 bpd while repairs are conducted. Estimated downtime is 12–18 days.

events.title: Iran crude exports hit 1.9Mbpd, highest since 2018, despite sanctions regime
events.summary: Tanker tracking data from multiple commercial providers indicates Iran's crude exports reached approximately 1.9 million bpd in April 2026, the highest level since the re-imposition of US sanctions in 2018. The bulk of flows are destined for independent Chinese refineries (teapots). US enforcement actions are ongoing but analysts note a structural gap between designation activity and actual export reduction.

events.title: Russia increases Arctic crude shipments by 40% as Baltic options narrow
events.summary: Tanker tracking data shows a 40% month-on-month increase in Russian crude shipments via Arctic Northern Sea routes, as G7 sanctions and port access restrictions continue to limit Baltic export options. Russian Arctic shipments now account for an estimated 8% of total Russian seaborne crude exports, up from under 2% in 2024. Dedicated ice-class tankers have been contracted from non-Western operators.
